trigger: BGP UPDATE withdraws a prefix or the BGP session goes down

steps.action: Receives BGP UPDATE message containing a withdrawn prefix (or detects session drop), removes the path_info from the adj-RIB-in, and calls bgp_process() to trigger a new best-path selection. With no remaining path, bgpd sends a ZAPI ROUTE_DELETE message to zebra via the internal ZAPI socket.
steps.output: BGP path entry removed from FRR BGP RIB; ZAPI delete message queued to zebra
steps.verify: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bgp ipv4 unicast <prefix>' (prefix should disappear or show no best path)

steps.action: Receives ZAPI ROUTE_DELETE from bgpd. rib_process() selects a new FIB entry (none in this case). rib_process_del_fib() calls rib_uninstall_kernel() which enqueues a DPLANE_OP_ROUTE_DELETE context to the dataplane. The dplane_fpm_nl module encodes an RTM_DELROUTE netlink message and writes it to the FPM TCP socket (port 2620) via fpm_nl_enqueue().
steps.output: Route removed from zebra RIB and Linux kernel routing table; RTM_DELROUTE FPM message sent to fpmsyncd
steps.verify: ip route show <prefix> (route should be absent)

steps.action: Receives the RTM_DELROUTE netlink message from zebra over the FPM TCP socket. onMsg() dispatches to onRouteMsg(), which detects nlmsg_type == RTM_DELROUTE and calls delWithWarmRestart() → ProducerStateTable::del() to remove the key from APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE.

ConsumerStateTable notifies orchagent of the DEL event on APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE. RouteOrch::doTask() reads the DEL_COMMAND and calls removeRoute() to stage a bulk remove of the SAI route entry via gRouteBulker.remove_entry(). After gRouteBulker.flush(), removeRoutePost() confirms SAI_STATUS_SUCCESS, removes the entry from m_syncdRoutes, and decrements next-hop-group reference counts.

Reads the BULK_REMOVE (or REMOVE) notification for SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY from ASIC_DB. processSingleEvent() dispatches to processBulkQuadEvent(SAI_COMMON_API_BULK_REMOVE) → processBulkRemoveEntry(), which calls m_vendorSai->bulkRemove() to invoke the vendor SAI remove_route_entries() API, deleting the route from the ASIC FIB/TCAM.
steps.output: Route entry removed from hardware forwarding table; packets to this prefix are no longer forwarded
steps.verify: show ip route <prefix> (should not appear; traffic to prefix should be dropped)

failure_injection_points.failure: zebra does not receive or process the ZAPI ROUTE_DELETE from bgpd
failure_injection_points.symptom: Prefix absent in 'show bgp ipv4 unicast' but still present in 'show ip route' and Linux kernel routing table — stale kernel route, traffic still forwarded to (now-invalid) next-hop
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status zebra; check /var/log/frr/zebra.log for ZAPI errors; restart frr container if zebra is crashed

failure_injection_points.failure: FPM socket disconnected or fpmsyncd crash — RTM_DELROUTE never reaches fpmsyncd
failure_injection_points.symptom: Route absent in 'ip route show' (kernel) but APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE key still present — stale APPL_DB entry; orchagent does not remove ASIC entry; traffic blackholed via hardware FIB pointing to removed next-hop
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec bgp supervisorctl status fpmsyncd; ss -tnp | grep 2620 (FPM TCP port should show ESTABLISHED); check /var/log/swss/fpmsyncd.log; restart fpmsyncd to re-drain zebra FPM buffer

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent is stuck or restarting — DEL event in APPL_DB not consumed
failure_injection_points.symptom: APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE key deleted but ASIC_DB route entry still present — hardware continues to forward traffic to withdrawn prefix (stale FIB entry, potential routing loop or traffic to dead next-hop)
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for SAI or consumer errors; redis-cli -n 0 XLEN APPL_DB_ROUTE_TABLE_KEY_SET to check pending entries

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd not processing ASIC_DB DEL — syncd crash, SAI error, or ASIC API failure
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB route entry deleted but hardware FIB still programmed — traffic forwarded to withdrawn prefix until next ASIC reset; syncd log shows SAI_STATUS_FAILURE on remove
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; check /var/log/syncd.log for SAI error codes; check ASIC vendor support logs; if persistent, warm-reboot or cold-reboot to resync hardware state
